---
title: ""
---


![](logo-bioquanti_fullname.png){width="40%" fig-align="left"}


|     Implemented in October 2022, the **bioQuanti** website was designed to contribute to reproducible and self-instructional teaching. *Reproducible teaching* is understood to be an active teaching-learning methodology based on programming codes, inherited from Donald Knuth's concepts of [literate programming](https://academic.oup.com/comjnl/article-abstract/27/2/97/343244?login=true) and reproducible research*, and aimed at producing and sharing dynamic documents and teaching objects. Originally conceived for the study of topics in *Quantitative Biochemistry* using programming codes, the site now has content for basic education, also based on programming languages.

|   In general, the website uses three programs, one for graphics, analysis and tables, simulations and animations ([R` & RStudio](https://bioquanti.netlify.app/uploads/biqbook/)), another for the study and three-dimensional visualization of atomic models ([Jmol](https://bioquanti.netlify.app/uploads/jmolbook/)), and a third for the dynamic observation of the structure and interaction of elements in networks ([SISMA](https://bioquanti.netlify.app/uploads/sismabook/)), this one developed at UNIFAL-MG. The detailed contents of the portal, as well as its application to higher education, can be seen [here](https://www.bioquimica.org.br/index.php/REB/article/view/1058). 

|   The portal has 4 freely distributed *ebooks* for studies in Biochemistry and related areas, namely: [Dynamic Visualization in Catalysis & Metabolism](http://dx.doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.25027.92964), [Quantitative Biochemistry & R](http://dx.doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.36090.40644), [Basic, People, Animal, and Plant Biochemistry](http://dx.doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.32623.55208), and [Jmol-Guided Structural Biochemistry](http://dx.doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.29268.10882). 

|   The website was awarded 2nd place in the Bayardo Baptista Torres National Prize for Biochemistry and Molecular Biology Teaching, promoted by the Brazilian Society of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology (SBBq, 2023), and is listed as an open educational resource on the [eduCAPES portal](https://educapes.capes.gov.br/handle/capes/748598). 

[![](logo_premioBayardo.png){width="15%"}](https://www2.sbbq.org.br/reuniao/reuniao-2023.html)

|   The site **bioQuanti** currently has higher education topics for Biochemistry, Physical Chemistry, Chemistry, Biophysics, Descriptive and Inferential Statistics, Experimental Planning, Linear Algebra, Integral Calculus, Differential Calculus, computational modeling, molecular modeling, among others; it has language aimed at university professors and researchers, and at undergraduate and postgraduate levels.

### **BioQuanti** for basic education

|   For basic education, the site offers a range of instructional content and teaching objects for the final years of primary and secondary education. Its content has also been designed under the principles of *reproducible teaching* (RE) and involves interactive teaching objects for curricular content (*OIER*). The site has a tutorial on the practical use of *Jmol* for 3D molecules and interactivity, and another for *R in the RStudio* environment, for interactive graphics and maps. The tools are presented in their cloud versions, eliminating the need for installation or configuration, although there is guidance for installing the program on a machine. The tutorial is illustrated with examples taken from *MAPA (Pedagogical Support Material for Learning in Minas Gerais)*. The portal also offers a collection of interactive atomic models included in a *“Garden of Molecules”* and a repository for interactive graphics and maps such as OIERs. The *Garden of Molecules* is organized into classes, and has a collection of atomic models for visualization and three-dimensional study.
